title: "This, My Friend June 2024"
startDate: "2024-06-21T00:00:00.000Z"
endDate: "2024-06-23T00:00:00.000Z"
destinations:
  - route: content/routes/this-my-friend.mdx
---

## Snow

As of 6/19... seems like it'd be ok with just microspikes.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/ColchuckCropped.MARUaLY-1024x869.jpg"

ot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:532,&quot;y&quot;:460},{&quot;x&quot;:533,&quot;y&quot;:438},{&quot;x&quot;:541,&quot;y&quot;:414},{&quot;x&quot;:543,&quot;y&quot;:398},{&quot;x&quot;:549,&quot;y&quot;:382},{&quot;x&quot;:564,&quot;y&quot;:362},{&quot;x&quot;:556,&quot;y&quot;:352}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:4,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:2,&quot;x&quot;:532,&quot;y&quot;:460,&quot;w&quot;:18,&quot;h&quot;:18,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:530,&quot;y&quot;:465},{&quot;x&quot;:522,&quot;y&quot;:476},{&quot;x&quot;:524,&quot;y&quot;:510},{&quot;x&quot;:528,&quot;y&quot;:532},{&quot;x&quot;:532,&quot;y&quot;:550},{&quot;x&quot;:538,&quot;y&quot;:567},{&quot;x&quot;:538,&quot;y&quot;:581},{&quot;x&quot;:532,&quot;y&quot;:594}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:4,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:2,&quot;x&quot;:520,&quot;y&quot;:624,&quot;w&quot;:18,&quot;h&quot;:18,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:545,&quot;y&quot;:613,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;P1 For context, on May 10th, 2015, Aasgard Pass also had ~2x more snow

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/11252763_10206660741059447_1596541739268820750_o.DHspbVY-1024x683.jpg)

6/10 satellite...

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/image.9PxCXY5X.png)

6/10 with slope

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/image.9iNDW5hX.png)

Google Satellite

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/image.2kEDgZgM.png)

## Itinerary

Saturday (4:24 AM dawn, 5:05 AM sunrise, 9:04 PM sunset, 9:45 PM dusk)

- 4:45 AM - Hike 5.5 miles, 4,100 ft gain (3 hours) (49°F, 6 mph winds, 61% cloud cover)
- 7:45 AM - Base of climb (51°F, 6 mph winds, 68% cloud cover)
- 8:15 AM - Climbing (5 pitches, 30 min/pitch, 2.5 hours)
- 10:45 AM - Top (53°F, 9 mph winds, 58% cloud cover)
- 11:15 AM - Descend and go over to Dragontail proper
- 12:00 PM - Dragontail (54°F, 9 mph winds, 58% cloud cover)
- 12:30 PM - Go back to car (~6 miles, ~4,400 ft descent, 3.5 hours)
- 4:00 PM - Car (69°F, 7 mph winds, 48% cloud cover)

Pushing schedule back 1 hour would make it about 2 degrees warmer while still avoiding the windiest part of the day (wind starts picking up at 2pm and picks up a lot more at 5pm).

Pushing back a second hour makes it about 4 degrees warmer than initial schedule, but starts to get closer to when it gets windier.

## Descent
